symptom: web-02 p99 latency crossed 138.4ms against a 100ms threshold, a +480% deviation from the 25ms baseline

evidence.statement: Config change CHG-4212 cut db-01 max_connections from 500 to 150 at 14:30:30Z, 90s before the connection-count onset
Alert ALT-9002 fired on db-01 connection_count reaching the new ceiling of 150 at 14:31:40Z
app-03 latency spiked to 178.3ms (ALT-9003) immediately downstream of db-01 on the topology path to web-02
No SNMP interface error counters available for tor-sw-03 to fully rule out a concurrent network-layer contribution

hypotheses.counterfactual: Removing config change CHG-4212 (restoring max_connections to 500) prevents the connection-pool exhaustion on db-01 and the downstream latency spike on web-02 in replay

evidence.statement: Config change CHG-4213 landed on web-05 at 14:31:50Z, only 10s before onset, closer in time than the true cause
Topology twin shows no dependency path from web-05 to symptom component web-02; candidate rejected by the topology-constrained causal filter
Alert ALT-9001 recorded a cache latency spike on cache-02 at 14:31:05Z, 55s before onset
web-02's dependency chain routes through cache-01, not cache-02; no dependency path to the symptom component exists

timeline.description: Cache eviction policy changed to allkeys-lru (CHG-4211)
Cache latency alert fired, 4.2ms observed against 3.0ms threshold
Connection ceiling alert fired, 150 connections observed against 145 threshold
Change-point onset detected on connection_count, 6.2 MAD above baseline
Latency critical alert fired, 178.3ms observed against 80ms threshold
Latency spike propagated to the web tier, p99 crossed the 100ms critical threshold

narrative: At 14:30:30Z an operator change (CHG-4212) cut db-01's connection pool from 500 to 150. Ninety seconds later the connection count breached the new ceiling, tripping a change-point onset on db-01. The topology-constrained causal engine traced a live dependency path from db-01 through app-03 to web-02, matching the order and timing of the downstream alerts. Two other events looked suspicious in isolation — a config push on web-05 ten seconds before onset, and a cache latency blip on cache-02 — but neither has a dependency path to web-02 in the topology twin, so both were demoted rather than blamed on proximity alone.
recommended_steps: Revert CHG-4212 to restore db-01 max_connections to 500 and confirm the latency spike on web-02 resolves
Pull SNMP interface error counters for tor-sw-03 to fully rule out a concurrent network-layer contribution
Re-run the topology shortestPath query for web-05 and cache-02 against the full mesh to confirm no indirect dependency path was missed
